questions.question: Match each object detector to its key characteristic.
categories: Single-pass detection treating it as a regression problem
Two-stage detection with a Region Proposal Network
Multi-scale predictions from different layers of a single network
Symmetric encoder-decoder for pixel-level segmentation

correct_mapping.YOLO: Single-pass detection treating it as a regression problem
correct_mapping.Faster R-CNN: Two-stage detection with a Region Proposal Network
correct_mapping.SSD: Multi-scale predictions from different layers of a single network

questions.explanation: YOLO predicts everything in one pass, making it fast. Faster R-CNN first proposes regions with an RPN, then classifies them, making it more accurate but slower. SSD makes predictions at multiple scales within a single network. U-Net is a segmentation model, not a detector, with an encoder-decoder structure and skip connections.

questions.question: Match each computer vision task to what it outputs.
categories: Bounding boxes with class labels around objects
A class label for every pixel, no instance distinction
A class label and unique ID for every pixel of each object
A single class label for the entire image

correct_mapping.Object detection: Bounding boxes with class labels around objects
correct_mapping.Semantic segmentation: A class label for every pixel, no instance distinction
correct_mapping.Instance segmentation: A class label and unique ID for every pixel of each object
correct_mapping.Image classification: A single class label for the entire image

questions.explanation: These tasks form a hierarchy of increasing detail. Classification gives one label per image. Detection localizes objects with boxes. Semantic segmentation labels every pixel by class. Instance segmentation goes furthest by separating individual objects at the pixel level.

questions.question: Match each data augmentation technique to what it does.
categories: Pastes a patch from one image onto another, mixing labels by area
Blends two images together with a weighted average of pixels and labels
Randomly changes brightness, contrast, and saturation
Mirrors the image horizontally or vertically

correct_mapping.CutMix: Pastes a patch from one image onto another, mixing labels by area
correct_mapping.Mixup: Blends two images together with a weighted average of pixels and labels
correct_mapping.Color jitter: Randomly changes brightness, contrast, and saturation
correct_mapping.Random flipping: Mirrors the image horizontally or vertically

questions.explanation: CutMix cuts a rectangular region from one image and pastes it onto another, mixing labels proportionally to the area. Mixup blends entire images by averaging their pixels. Color jitter varies photometric properties to build robustness to lighting changes. Flipping creates mirror images to teach the model that objects can face either direction.

questions.question: Match each transfer learning strategy to the scenario where it works best.
items: Freeze backbone, train new head only
categories: Small target dataset, similar to the pretraining domain
Large target dataset, different domain from pretraining
Medium dataset, unfreeze layers one at a time to avoid forgetting
Massive unique dataset with no suitable pretrained model

correct_mapping.Freeze backbone, train new head only: Small target dataset, similar to the pretraining domain
correct_mapping.Fine-tune all layers: Large target dataset, different domain from pretraining
correct_mapping.Gradual unfreezing: Medium dataset, unfreeze layers one at a time to avoid forgetting
correct_mapping.Train from scratch: Massive unique dataset with no suitable pretrained model

questions.explanation: With little data and a similar domain, freezing the backbone avoids overfitting. With lots of data and a different domain, fine-tuning everything lets the model fully adapt. Gradual unfreezing balances adaptation and knowledge preservation. Training from scratch only makes sense with massive amounts of unique data.

questions.question: Match each Faster R-CNN component to its role.
categories: Extracts feature maps from the input image
Proposes candidate regions likely containing objects
Converts variable-size regions to fixed-size features
Predicts the object class and refines the bounding box

correct_mapping.Backbone CNN: Extracts feature maps from the input image
correct_mapping.Region Proposal Network: Proposes candidate regions likely containing objects
correct_mapping.ROI Pooling: Converts variable-size regions to fixed-size features
correct_mapping.Classification head: Predicts the object class and refines the bounding box

questions.explanation: Faster R-CNN is a pipeline: the backbone extracts features, the RPN suggests where objects might be, ROI Pooling standardizes the feature size for each proposal, and the classification head makes the final prediction for each proposed region.

questions.question: Match each ViT component to its purpose.
categories: Converts each image patch into a vector
Tells the model where each patch is located in the image
Collects global information for the final classification
Applies self-attention across all patches

correct_mapping.Patch embedding: Converts each image patch into a vector
correct_mapping.Position embeddings: Tells the model where each patch is located in the image
correct_mapping.[CLS] token: Collects global information for the final classification

questions.explanation: ViT first embeds each image patch into a vector. Position embeddings are added because self-attention has no built-in sense of spatial order. The [CLS] token aggregates information from all patches and is used for the final class prediction. The Transformer encoder captures relationships between patches via self-attention.

questions.question: Match each detection concept to its description.
categories: Measures overlap between predicted and ground truth boxes
Removes duplicate detections, keeping the most confident
Predefined reference boxes the model adjusts
Predicting offsets to refine box coordinates

correct_mapping.IoU: Measures overlap between predicted and ground truth boxes
correct_mapping.NMS: Removes duplicate detections, keeping the most confident
correct_mapping.Anchor boxes: Predefined reference boxes the model adjusts
correct_mapping.Bounding box regression: Predicting offsets to refine box coordinates

questions.explanation: IoU quantifies localization accuracy by measuring box overlap. NMS is a post-processing step that eliminates redundant detections. Anchor boxes provide starting references for predictions. Bounding box regression is the process of predicting adjustments to make boxes fit objects more precisely.

questions.question: Match each architecture to why it is notable.
categories: First successful pure-Transformer model for image classification
Multi-scale features for detecting objects of different sizes
Real-time object detection in a single forward pass
Extends Faster R-CNN to perform instance segmentation

correct_mapping.ViT: First successful pure-Transformer model for image classification
correct_mapping.FPN: Multi-scale features for detecting objects of different sizes
correct_mapping.YOLO: Real-time object detection in a single forward pass
correct_mapping.Mask R-CNN: Extends Faster R-CNN to perform instance segmentation

questions.explanation: ViT showed that Transformers can match CNNs for image tasks. FPN creates rich multi-scale features useful for detecting objects of varying sizes. YOLO pioneered fast, single-pass detection. Mask R-CNN added a mask prediction branch to Faster R-CNN, enabling pixel-level instance segmentation.

questions.question: Match each U-Net component to its function.
categories: Downsamples the image to capture context and semantic meaning
Upsamples features to restore the original resolution
Passes fine spatial details from encoder to decoder
Maps features to class predictions for each pixel

correct_mapping.Encoder (contracting path): Downsamples the image to capture context and semantic meaning
correct_mapping.Decoder (expanding path): Upsamples features to restore the original resolution
correct_mapping.Skip connections: Passes fine spatial details from encoder to decoder
correct_mapping.Final 1x1 convolution: Maps features to class predictions for each pixel

questions.explanation: The encoder progressively reduces spatial dimensions while building richer feature representations. The decoder upsamples back to the original resolution. Skip connections bridge corresponding encoder and decoder levels, preserving fine details that would otherwise be lost during downsampling. The final 1x1 convolution produces per-pixel class predictions.

questions.question: Match each speed-vs-accuracy tradeoff to the correct detector.
categories: Fastest, single-pass, may miss small objects
Slower, two-stage, generally more accurate
Fast single-pass with multi-scale predictions
Accurate multi-scale detection using feature pyramids

correct_mapping.YOLO: Fastest, single-pass, may miss small objects
correct_mapping.Two-stage with FPN: Accurate multi-scale detection using feature pyramids

questions.explanation: YOLO prioritizes speed with a single-pass approach but can struggle with small or closely packed objects. Faster R-CNN is more accurate due to its two-stage design but slower. SSD balances speed and scale handling by predicting from multiple feature map levels. Adding FPN to a two-stage detector combines multi-scale features with the accuracy of region-based classification.
